page: Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz and supported by a 320 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 937 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score and text processing tests reaching top-tier performance within the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong results in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is more limited, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with clang falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is mixed, showing average performance for small cached blocks but weaker performance for large uncached blocks.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and bundled local NVMe storage, the i7i.xlarge offers a resource-dense profile suitable for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads without the overhead of larger multi-core instances. It is well-suited for database operations, redis caching, and text processing tasks, though it is less optimal for highly parallelized multi-threaded compute tasks or heavy software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor. It provides 4 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a bundled 937 GB NVMe SSD. Operating with a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded decompression tasks. However, multi-core benchmarks and uncached memory bandwidth show weaker relative performance. With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast local storage, the i7i.xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is best suited for database operations, caching, and single-threaded processing, rather than highly parallelized compute tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 937 GB NVMe SSD, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and a bundled 937 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A storage-optimized server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing, DDR5 memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 320 MB L3 cache
32.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 937 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and text processing performance
Best suited for database operations, caching, and single-threaded tasks
